findings.title: Firestarter Backdoor — Cisco ASA/FTD Firmware-Level Persistence Survives Patches (CISA+NCSC Joint Alert)
findings.affected_technology: Cisco Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA), Cisco Firepower Threat Defense (FTD)
findings.summary: CISA and NCSC issued a joint alert after discovering that threat actor UAT-4356 deployed firmware-level backdoor malware on Cisco ASA/FTD firewalls that survives security patches, requiring full device reimaging for remediation.

findings.title: CVE-2026-32604 — Spinnaker Pre-Auth RCE via gitrepo Artifact Injection
findings.summary: A command injection in Spinnaker's gitrepo artifact handler lets a low-privilege pipeline user execute arbitrary commands on clouddriver pods, which typically hold AWS/GCP/Kubernetes service-account tokens for every environment the org deploys to.

findings.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Expands — Bitwarden CLI npm Package Compromised via Checkmarx GitHub Action
findings.summary: TeamPCP leveraged a compromised Checkmarx GitHub Action to inject credential-stealing malware with self-propagation capabilities into the Bitwarden CLI npm package for 90 minutes on April 22.

findings.title: Zimbra XSS Mass Exploitation — 10,000+ Exposed Servers Vulnerable to CVE-2025-66376
findings.summary: BleepingComputer quantified that over 10,000 internet-exposed Zimbra instances remain vulnerable to the November 2025 Classic UI stored XSS triggered via CSS @import in HTML mail, with ongoing exploitation tied to suspected Russian state actors.

findings.title: CVE-2026-41248 — Clerk JavaScript Middleware Bypass Across Next.js / Nuxt / Astro
findings.summary: Clerk's createRouteMatcher middleware gate can be bypassed with a crafted query string, silently exposing protected routes in any Next.js/Nuxt/Astro app that relies on Clerk middleware without additional server-side authorization checks.

findings.title: CVE-2026-33626 — LMDeploy Vision-Language Module SSRF Exploited Within 13 Hours of Disclosure
findings.summary: Server-side request forgery in LMDeploy's vision-language module enables unauthenticated access to cloud metadata services and internal networks, with exploitation observed within 13 hours of disclosure.

findings.title: CVE-2026-25895 — FUXA SCADA HMI Unauthenticated Path Traversal to RCE (PoC Public)
findings.summary: Public exploit chains unauthenticated path traversal with arbitrary file write to achieve remote code execution on internet-facing FUXA SCADA HMI servers.

findings.title: CVE-2026-41428 — Budibase Authentication Middleware Regex Bypass
findings.summary: Unanchored regex in Budibase's auth middleware lets any unauthenticated attacker reach protected endpoints by appending a public endpoint path as a query parameter.

findings.title: CVE-2026-41328 — Dgraph GraphQL Database Unauthenticated DQL Injection
findings.summary: DQL injection via the language-tag field lets an unauthenticated attacker exfiltrate the entire Dgraph database in default configurations where ACLs are disabled.

noted.summary: Vendor advisory GHSA-jp74-mfrx-3qvh now published; authenticated low-priv SQLi via mobile-sync routes in versions <1.4.6, 1.5.0-beta.0 through 1.5.5, 1.6.0-alpha.0 through 1.6.0-beta.4. CVSS 10.0.
Critical vulnerability (CVSS 9.9) reported by PT Security; no vendor advisory or technical details published yet.
Web hosting control panel vulnerability (CVSS 8.8); internet-facing by design, limited details available.
Authentication bypass via patient social-login REST endpoint in versions up to 4.1.2; PoC on GitHub.
Path traversal via symlink extraction enabling arbitrary file overwrite; supply chain implications for pipelines using npm tar.
Critical vulnerability patched with no public technical details; monitor for advisory disclosure.
High-severity flaw patched with no public technical details; monitor for advisory disclosure.
VU#748485: Unauthenticated configuration modification vulnerability published by CERT/CC on April 23.
GitHub Security Lab GHSL-2026-052 (Apr 24): stored XSS via MIME type spoofing in the wiki/collaboration platform.
GitHub Security Lab GHSL-2026-012 (Apr 24): unauthorized data exposure via REST API link expansion in Frappe-based deployments.
Deutsche Telekom Red Team disclosed a 12-year-old pkcon auth flaw letting local users escalate to root on Ubuntu/Debian/Rocky/Fedora; CVSS 8.8, fixed in PackageKit 1.3.5. Local-only.
Pre-auth buffer overflow RCE (CVSS 9.8) with active PoC on GitHub; sibling of previously-covered CVE-2026-24061.
